consolidation_steps.cross_check: Findings tagged with confidence based on reviewer agreement
consolidation_steps.deduplication: Same underlying issues merged; kept most specific description and highest severity
consolidation_steps.scoring: Starting at 100, apply severity weights: critical=-15, high=-8, medium=-3, low=-1

findings.description: Document references 'Section 6' for security considerations, but Section 6 does not exist in the document
findings.evidence: Line 37: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' No Section 6 appears anywhere in the 53-line document.
findings.suggestion: Either add Section 6 with security documentation or remove the reference

findings.description: Overview claims 'All pipeline stages run in parallel' but Stage 2 states 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins', creating a logical contradiction
findings.evidence: Line 5: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' vs Line 21: 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.'
findings.suggestion: Clarify the actual execution model: either fully parallel, partially parallel with Stage 2→3 dependency, or conditional parallel

findings.description: Document claims to comprehensively describe architecture but omits key sections: security (referenced but missing), detailed error handling, SLAs, and performance baseline comparisons
findings.evidence: Stages 1-4 present but 'Section 6' promised for security never delivered. Performance section cites benchmarks without methodology.
findings.suggestion: Either expand document to include all referenced/promised sections or revise scope

findings.description: Claims 'delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9%' is unsupported and contradicted by separate claim of 'sub-second end-to-end latency'; extreme claims lack methodology
findings.evidence: Line 29: 'reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9%' vs Line 43: 'maintains sub-second end-to-end latency' without explanation of baseline or relationship
findings.suggestion: Either provide benchmarking methodology and baseline, or remove/moderate unsupported claims

findings.description: Parallel execution claim in Overview contradicted by Stage 2 dependency; creates reader confusion about actual pipeline flow
findings.evidence: Line 5 states parallel execution; Line 21 describes Stage 2→3 dependency incompatible with parallel model
findings.suggestion: Provide single, consistent execution model description; update Overview if stages don't truly run parallel

findings.description: Inconsistent error reporting language: Ingestion uses HTTP-specific '400 response' while Stage 3 uses generic 'errors are logged' without status codes
findings.evidence: Line 13: 'rejected with a 400 response' vs Line 31: 'Errors are logged and the batch is requeued'
findings.suggestion: Standardize error reporting terminology and detail level across all stages

findings.description: Retry policy inconsistency: Stage 3 specifies '3 retry attempts' but Operational Notes don't confirm policy consistency across all stages
findings.evidence: Line 31: 'batch is requeued for up to 3 retry attempts' but no confirmation this applies to earlier stages' failures
findings.suggestion: Explicitly document retry policy as global across all stages, or specify per-stage policies

findings.description: Sequence of operations in Ingestion is ambiguous: validation happens 'on arrival' then authentication happens 'before processing begins'; relationship unclear
findings.evidence: Lines 13-15 describe validation and authentication in unclear order: 'Events are validated... Authentication is used to verify...' ordering suggests parallel or reversed sequence
findings.suggestion: Clarify exact sequence: HTTP POST → authenticate request → validate event schema → queue event

findings.description: Benchmarks cited without source attribution or methodology; '3× faster' claim unsupported
findings.evidence: Line 41: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming' with no source or methodology provided
findings.suggestion: Add benchmark methodology subsection or cite external source; remove unsupported claims

findings.description: Inconsistent polling intervals: Stage 3 uses 5-minute batch completion polling, Stage 4 uses 10-minute status polling; rationale not explained
findings.evidence: Line 31: '5-minute interval' for batch polling vs Line 35: '10-minute interval' for status polling without justification
findings.suggestion: Explain why intervals differ or standardize polling cadence

findings.description: 'Standard retry policy' mentioned but never defined or referenced; unclear what standard applies
findings.evidence: Line 23: 'Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures' with no definition
findings.suggestion: Define 'standard policy' (e.g., exponential backoff, max 3 retries) or reference documentation

findings.description: Performance claims lack context about load conditions, data types, or hardware configurations; seem disconnected from architecture description above
findings.evidence: Lines 41-43 cite benchmarks and latency claims without specifying load conditions, data sizes, or hardware assumptions
findings.suggestion: Either tie performance numbers to specific architecture configurations or remove unsupported claims

findings.description: Parallelization model contradicted: Overview claims parallel stages but content shows Stage 2→3 dependency
findings.evidence: Line 5: 'run in parallel' contradicted by Line 21: 'must complete before'
findings.suggestion: Verify and clarify parallelization model in Overview

findings.description: Statement 'add worker instances to increase throughput' is obvious from architecture; wastes space or should be expanded with specific guidance
findings.evidence: Line 49: 'Horizontal scaling is supported; add worker instances to increase throughput' provides no new insight
findings.suggestion: Remove or expand with specific scaling guidance (instance types, max nodes, cost impacts)

findings.description: Terminology varies: uses 'events', 'records', 'payloads', 'batches' sometimes interchangeably without clear distinction
findings.evidence: Lines use: 'analytics events' (line 3), 'Event records' (line 13), 'records' (line 21), 'batches of records' (line 29)
findings.suggestion: Standardize terminology: 'events' for input, 'records' for transformed data, 'batches' for delivery units

findings.description: Mentions 'Future work includes adding a real-time streaming workflow' which is scope creep beyond current architecture description
findings.evidence: Line 53: Future work statement doesn't belong in current architecture brief; introduces unimplemented scope
findings.suggestion: Remove or develop streaming architecture as separate document

findings.description: Tone shifts between formal architecture specification language and operational deployment guide language
findings.evidence: Architecture sections (1-4) formal vs Operational Notes (line 47-49) more conversational
findings.suggestion: Either separate into architecture spec + operational runbook or maintain consistent formality
